title: Document the structure of auth.json

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

The documentation, if any, should probably be in in the containers/image repository that maintains the code. To an extent https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/commandline/login applies.

But maybe you are actually more interested in a good way to add credentials to that file. Currently the best way is `podman login`, but there’s already work in progress to add `login`/`logout` to Skopeo: https://github.com/containers/skopeo/pull/865

I'll let @mtrmac have the final say, but if you're suggesting adding something to the README.md pointing out its use and a pointer to its man page, I'd be OK with that.  Just in case you haven't stumbled across it, the man page lives in [containers/image](https://github.com/containers/image/blob/fe9e1741e53189457e49f8014b8d4c79f4fc32fe/docs/containers-auth.json.5.md)

Oh, there already is a man page. Silly me.

Anyway, I’d prefer to point at `skopeo login`, when it arrives, rather than a script that does that  manually.
@sarcasticadmin Glad that helped.  I think what you're suggesting to add could be helpful.  I'd say open a PR and we can discuss in there.
A link to `containers-auth.json(5)` was added to `skopeo-login(1)` and `skopeo-logout(1)` in #914.
